title: Commentary on Key Financial Performance – Q1'26
content:
Northern Valley financial position continues to be supported by exceptionally strong capital levels. The credit union reports a net worth ratio of 14.34%, representing an improvement of 159.30 bps from the prior period, and a solvency ratio of 1.17%. This robust buffer provides ample room for absorbing potential losses while maintaining regulatory standing.

Asset quality remains stable and manageable. The delinquency ratio stands at 0.24% of total loans, while net charge-offs stand at -0.05% of average loans. Delinquency has improved by 13.40 bps from the prior period, reflecting tighter collection efforts or reduced exposure to high-risk segments.

Profitability remains positive. The credit union reports a return on average assets (ROAA) of 0.93% and a net interest margin of 3.91%, both declining by -5.33 bps respectively from the prior period, driven primarily by rising funding costs that compressed spreads despite stable loan yields.

Balance-sheet structure continues to reflect an adequately leveraged operating profile. Loans represent 77.31% of shares and have declined by -44.73%, while shares and deposits have also contracted significantly at -38.22%. This reflects continued balance-sheet contraction, with core funding ratio holding steady at 51.96%, demonstrating prudent liquidity management amid shrinking asset base.

Overall, Northern Valley enters Q1'26 with resilient capitalization but faces headwinds in profitability and growth due to member attrition and loan runoff. The institution’s primary strength lies in its well-capitalized position, which will be critical for sustaining operations through the current contraction phase.
